[rustls/Format                                         ] ⭐ Run Main Install rust toolchain

[rustls/Format                                         ] ⭐ Run Main : parse toolchain version
if [[ -z $toolchain ]]; then
  # GitHub does not enforce `required: true` inputs itself. https://github.com/actions/runner/issues/1070
  echo "'toolchain' is a required input" >&2
  exit 1
elif [[ $toolchain =~ ^stable' '[0-9]+' '(year|month|week|day)s?' 'ago$ ]]; then
  if [[ Linux == macOS ]]; then

[rustls/audit                                          ]   | [0m[36m23[0m [0m[36m│[0m [0m[31maws-lc-fips-sys 0.13.11 registry+https://github.com/rust-lang/crates.io-index[0m
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |    [0m[36m│[0m [0m[31m━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━[0m [0m[31msecurity vulnerability detected[0m
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |    [0m[36m│[0m
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |    [0m[36m├[0m ID: RUSTSEC-2026-0042
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |    [0m[36m├[0m Advisory: https://rustsec.org/advisories/RUSTSEC-2026-0042
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |    [0m[36m├[0m A logic error in CRL distribution point matching in AWS-LC allows a revoked
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |      certificate to bypass revocation checks during certificate validation, when
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |      the application enables CRL checking and uses partitioned CRLs with Issuing
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |      Distribution Point (IDP) extensions.
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |      
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |      Customers of AWS services do not need to take action. `aws-lc-fips-sys`
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |      contains code from AWS-LC. Applications using `aws-lc-fips-sys` should
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |      upgrade to the most recent release of `aws-lc-fips-sys`.
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |      
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |      ## Workarounds
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |      
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |      Applications can workaround this issue if they do not enable CRL checking
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |      (`X509_V_FLAG_CRL_CHECK`). Applications using complete (non-partitioned)
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |      CRLs without IDP extensions are also not affected.
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |      
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |      Otherwise, there is no workaround and applications using `aws-lc-fips-sys`
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |      should upgrade to the most recent releases of `aws-lc-fips-sys`.
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |    [0m[36m├[0m Announcement: https://aws.amazon.com/security/security-bulletins/2026-010-AWS
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |    [0m[36m├[0m Solution: Upgrade to >=0.13.13 (try `cargo update -p aws-lc-fips-sys`)

[rustls/audit                                          ]   |    [0m[36m├[0m Observable timing discrepancy in AES-CCM decryption in AWS-LC allows an
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |      unauthenticated user to potentially determine authentication tag validity
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |      via timing analysis.
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |      
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |      The impacted implementations are through the EVP CIPHER API:
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |      `EVP_aes_128_ccm`, `EVP_aes_192_ccm`, and `EVP_aes_256_ccm`.
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |      
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |      Customers of AWS services do not need to take action. `aws-lc-fips-sys`
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |      contains code from AWS-LC. Applications using `aws-lc-fips-sys` should
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |      upgrade to the most recent release of `aws-lc-fips-sys`.
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |      
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |      ## Workarounds
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |      
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |      In the special cases of using AES-CCM with (M=4, L=2), (M=8, L=2), or
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |      (M=16, L=2), applications can workaround this issue by using AES-CCM
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |      through the EVP AEAD API using implementations
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |      `EVP_aead_aes_128_ccm_bluetooth`, `EVP_aead_aes_128_ccm_bluetooth_8`,
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |      and `EVP_aead_aes_128_ccm_matter` respectively.
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |      
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |      Otherwise, there is no workaround and applications using `aws-lc-fips-sys`
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |      should upgrade to the most recent release of `aws-lc-fips-sys`.
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |    [0m[36m├[0m Announcement: https://aws.amazon.com/security/security-bulletins/2026-005-AWS
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |    [0m[36m├[0m Solution: Upgrade to >=0.13.12 (try `cargo update -p aws-lc-fips-sys`)
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |    [0m[36m├[0m aws-lc-fips-sys v0.13.11
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |      └── aws-lc-rs v1.16.0

[rustls/audit                                          ]   | [0m[1m[38;5;9merror[vulnerability][0m[1m: AWS-LC X.509 Name Constraints Bypass via Wildcard/Unicode CN[0m
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |    [0m[36m┌─[0m /mnt/4tbc/home/mnabil/LLM4Build/project_path/rustls/Cargo.lock:25:1
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |    [0m[36m│[0m
[rustls/audit                                          ]   | [0m[36m25[0m [0m[36m│[0m [0m[31maws-lc-sys 0.37.1 registry+https://github.com/rust-lang/crates.io-index[0m
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |    [0m[36m│[0m [0m[31m━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━[0m [0m[31msecurity vulnerability detected[0m
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |    [0m[36m│[0m
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |    [0m[36m├[0m ID: RUSTSEC-2026-0044
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |    [0m[36m├[0m Advisory: https://rustsec.org/advisories/RUSTSEC-2026-0044
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |    [0m[36m├[0m A logic error in CN (Common Name) validation allows certificates with
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |      wildcard or raw UTF-8 Unicode CN values to bypass name constraints
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |      enforcement. The `cn2dnsid` function does not recognize these CN patterns
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |      as valid DNS identifiers, causing `NAME_CONSTRAINTS_check_CN` to skip
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |      validation. However, `X509_check_host` accepts these CN values when no
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |      dNSName SAN is present, allowing certificates to bypass name constraints
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |      while still being used for hostname verification.
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |      
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |      Customers of AWS services do not need to take action. Applications using
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |      `aws-lc-sys` should upgrade to the most recent release of `aws-lc-sys`.
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |      
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |      ## Workarounds
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |      
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |      Applications that set `X509_CHECK_FLAG_NEVER_CHECK_SUBJECT` to disable CN
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |      fallback are not affected. Applications that only encounter certificates
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |      with dNSName SANs (standard for public WebPKI) are also not affected.
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |      
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |      Otherwise, there is no workaround and applications using `aws-lc-sys` should
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |      upgrade to the most recent releases of `aws-lc-sys`.
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |    [0m[36m├[0m Solution: Upgrade to >=0.39.0 (try `cargo update -p aws-lc-sys`)
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |    [0m[36m├[0m aws-lc-sys v0.37.1
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |      └── aws-lc-rs v1.16.0

[rustls/audit                                          ]   |    [0m[36m│[0m [0m[31m━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━[0m [0m[31msecurity vulnerability detected[0m
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |    [0m[36m│[0m
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |    [0m[36m├[0m ID: RUSTSEC-2026-0048
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |    [0m[36m├[0m Advisory: https://rustsec.org/advisories/RUSTSEC-2026-0048
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |    [0m[36m├[0m A logic error in CRL distribution point matching in AWS-LC allows a revoked
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |      certificate to bypass revocation checks during certificate validation, when
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |      the application enables CRL checking and uses partitioned CRLs with Issuing
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |      Distribution Point (IDP) extensions.
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |      
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |      Customers of AWS services do not need to take action. `aws-lc-sys` contains
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |      code from AWS-LC. Applications using `aws-lc-sys` should upgrade to the most
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |      recent release of `aws-lc-sys`.
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |      
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |      ## Workarounds
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |      
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |      Applications can workaround this issue if they do not enable CRL checking
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |      (`X509_V_FLAG_CRL_CHECK`). Applications using complete (non-partitioned)
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |      CRLs without IDP extensions are also not affected.
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |      
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |      Otherwise, there is no workaround and applications using `aws-lc-sys` should
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |      upgrade to the most recent releases of `aws-lc-sys`.
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |    [0m[36m├[0m Announcement: https://aws.amazon.com/security/security-bulletins/2026-010-AWS
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |    [0m[36m├[0m Solution: Upgrade to >=0.39.0 (try `cargo update -p aws-lc-sys`)
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |    [0m[36m├[0m aws-lc-sys v0.37.1

[rustls/audit                                          ]   | [0m[1m[38;5;9merror[vulnerability][0m[1m: Incorrect Output of Incremental Portable SHAKE API[0m
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |     [0m[36m┌─[0m /mnt/4tbc/home/mnabil/LLM4Build/project_path/rustls/Cargo.lock:162:1
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |     [0m[36m│[0m
[rustls/audit                                          ]   | [0m[36m162[0m [0m[36m│[0m [0m[31mlibcrux-sha3 0.0.6 registry+https://github.com/rust-lang/crates.io-index[0m
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |     [0m[36m│[0m [0m[31m━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━[0m [0m[31msecurity vulnerability detected[0m
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |     [0m[36m│[0m
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |     [0m[36m├[0m ID: RUSTSEC-2026-0074
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |     [0m[36m├[0m Advisory: https://rustsec.org/advisories/RUSTSEC-2026-0074
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |     [0m[36m├[0m The incremental squeeze functions in the portable SHAKE XOF API, when
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |        attempting to squeeze more than `RATE` (168 for SHAKE128, 136 for
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |        SHAKE256) bytes, performed an additional permutation of the state
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |        before producing the first output block, thus discarding the first
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |        block of `RATE` bytes of valid XOF output.
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |       
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |       ## Impact
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |       This bug impacts users that rely on this XOF API to squeeze more than
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |       `RATE` bytes. It does not impact the use of libcrux-sha3 in
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |       libcrux-ml-kem or libcrux-ml-dsa.
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |       
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |       ## Mitigation
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |       Starting from version `0.0.8` the squeeze functions correctly output
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |       all blocks including the first block.
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |     [0m[36m├[0m Announcement: https://github.com/cryspen/libcrux/pull/1352
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |     [0m[36m├[0m Solution: Upgrade to >=0.0.8 (try `cargo update -p libcrux-sha3`)
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |     [0m[36m├[0m libcrux-sha3 v0.0.6
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |       ├── hpke-rs v0.6.0
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |       │   └── rustls-provider-example v0.0.1
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |       │       └── rustls-provider-test v0.1.0
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |       └── libcrux-ml-kem v0.0.6
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |           └── rustls-graviola v0.3.3
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |               └── rustls-bench v0.1.0
[rustls/audit                                          ]   | 
[rustls/audit                                          ]   | [0m[1m[38;5;9merror[vulnerability][0m[1m: CRLs not considered authoritative by Distribution Point due to faulty matching logic[0m
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |     [0m[36m┌─[0m /mnt/4tbc/home/mnabil/LLM4Build/project_path/rustls/Cargo.lock:262:1
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |     [0m[36m│[0m
[rustls/audit                                          ]   | [0m[36m262[0m [0m[36m│[0m [0m[31mrustls-webpki 0.103.9 registry+https://github.com/rust-lang/crates.io-index[0m
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |     [0m[36m│[0m [0m[31m━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━[0m [0m[31msecurity vulnerability detected[0m
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |     [0m[36m│[0m
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |     [0m[36m├[0m ID: RUSTSEC-2026-0049
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |     [0m[36m├[0m Advisory: https://rustsec.org/advisories/RUSTSEC-2026-0049
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |     [0m[36m├[0m If a certificate had more than one `distributionPoint`, then only the first `distributionPoint` would be considered against each CRL's `IssuingDistributionPoint` `distributionPoint`, and then the certificate's subsequent `distributionPoint`s would be ignored.
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |       
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |       The impact was that correctly provided CRLs would not be consulted to check revocation. With `UnknownStatusPolicy::Deny` (the default) this would lead to incorrect but safe `Error::UnknownRevocationStatus`. With `UnknownStatusPolicy::Allow` this would lead to inappropriate acceptance of revoked certificates.
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |       
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |       This vulnerability is thought to be of limited impact. This is because both the certificate and CRL are signed -- an attacker would need to compromise a trusted issuing authority to trigger this bug.  An attacker with such capabilities could likely bypass revocation checking through other more impactful means (such as publishing a valid, empty CRL.)
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |       
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |       More likely, this bug would be latent in normal use, and an attacker could leverage faulty revocation checking to continue using a revoked credential.
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |       
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |       This vulnerability is identified as [GHSA-pwjx-qhcg-rvj4](https://github.com/rustls/webpki/security/advisories/GHSA-pwjx-qhcg-rvj4). Thank you to @1seal for the report.
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |     [0m[36m├[0m Solution: Upgrade to >=0.103.10 (try `cargo update -p rustls-webpki`)
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |     [0m[36m├[0m rustls-webpki v0.103.9
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |       └── rustls v0.23.36
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |           ├── hickory-proto v0.25.2
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |           │   └── hickory-resolver v0.25.2
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |           │       └── rustls-examples v0.0.1
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |           ├── hickory-resolver v0.25.2 (*)
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |           ├── rustls-graviola v0.3.3
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |           │   └── rustls-bench v0.1.0
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |           ├── rustls-post-quantum v0.2.4
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |           └── tokio-rustls v0.26.4
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |               ├── hickory-proto v0.25.2 (*)

[rustls/audit                                          ]   | [0m[1m[38;5;9merror[vulnerability][0m[1m: Name constraints for URI names were incorrectly accepted[0m
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |     [0m[36m┌─[0m /mnt/4tbc/home/mnabil/LLM4Build/project_path/rustls/Cargo.lock:262:1
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |     [0m[36m│[0m
[rustls/audit                                          ]   | [0m[36m262[0m [0m[36m│[0m [0m[31mrustls-webpki 0.103.9 registry+https://github.com/rust-lang/crates.io-index[0m
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |     [0m[36m│[0m [0m[31m━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━[0m [0m[31msecurity vulnerability detected[0m
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |     [0m[36m│[0m
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |     [0m[36m├[0m ID: RUSTSEC-2026-0098
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |     [0m[36m├[0m Advisory: https://rustsec.org/advisories/RUSTSEC-2026-0098
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |     [0m[36m├[0m Name constraints for URI names were ignored and therefore accepted.
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |       
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |       Note this library does not provide an API for asserting URI names, and URI name constraints are otherwise not implemented.  URI name constraints are now rejected unconditionally.
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |       
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |       Since name constraints are restrictions on otherwise properly-issued certificates, this bug is reachable only after signature verification and requires misissuance to exploit.
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |       
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |       This vulnerability is identified as [GHSA-965h-392x-2mh5](https://github.com/rustls/webpki/security/advisories/GHSA-965h-392x-2mh5). Thank you to @1seal for the report.
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |     [0m[36m├[0m Solution: Upgrade to >=0.103.12, <0.104.0-alpha.1 OR >=0.104.0-alpha.6 (try `cargo update -p rustls-webpki`)

[rustls/audit                                          ]   | [0m[1m[38;5;9merror[vulnerability][0m[1m: Name constraints were accepted for certificates asserting a wildcard name[0m
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |     [0m[36m┌─[0m /mnt/4tbc/home/mnabil/LLM4Build/project_path/rustls/Cargo.lock:262:1
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |     [0m[36m│[0m
[rustls/audit                                          ]   | [0m[36m262[0m [0m[36m│[0m [0m[31mrustls-webpki 0.103.9 registry+https://github.com/rust-lang/crates.io-index[0m
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |     [0m[36m│[0m [0m[31m━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━[0m [0m[31msecurity vulnerability detected[0m
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |     [0m[36m│[0m
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |     [0m[36m├[0m ID: RUSTSEC-2026-0099
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |     [0m[36m├[0m Advisory: https://rustsec.org/advisories/RUSTSEC-2026-0099
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |     [0m[36m├[0m Permitted subtree name constraints for DNS names were accepted for certificates asserting a wildcard name.
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |       
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |       This was incorrect because, given a name constraint of `accept.example.com`, `*.example.com` could feasibly allow a name of `reject.example.com` which is outside the constraint.
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |       This is very similar to [CVE-2025-61727](https://go.dev/issue/76442).
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |       
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |       Since name constraints are restrictions on otherwise properly-issued certificates, this bug is reachable only after signature verification and requires misissuance to exploit.
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |       
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |       This vulnerability is identified as [GHSA-xgp8-3hg3-c2mh](https://github.com/rustls/webpki/security/advisories/GHSA-xgp8-3hg3-c2mh). Thank you to @1seal for the report.
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |     [0m[36m├[0m Solution: Upgrade to >=0.103.12, <0.104.0-alpha.1 OR >=0.104.0-alpha.6 (try `cargo update -p rustls-webpki`)
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |     [0m[36m├[0m rustls-webpki v0.103.9

[rustls/audit                                          ]   |           ├── hickory-proto v0.25.2

[rustls/audit                                          ]   |           ├── hickory-resolver v0.25.2 (*)

[rustls/audit                                          ]   |       
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |       Note this library does not provide an API for asserting URI names, and URI name constraints are otherwise not implemented.  URI name constraints are now rejected unconditionally.
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |       
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |       Since name constraints are restrictions on otherwise properly-issued certificates, this bug is reachable only after signature verification and requires misissuance to exploit.

[rustls/audit                                          ]   | [0m[36m263[0m [0m[36m│[0m [0m[31mrustls-webpki 0.104.0-alpha.2 registry+https://github.com/rust-lang/crates.io-index[0m
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |     [0m[36m│[0m [0m[31m━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━[0m [0m[31msecurity vulnerability detected[0m
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |     [0m[36m│[0m
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |     [0m[36m├[0m ID: RUSTSEC-2026-0099
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |     [0m[36m├[0m Advisory: https://rustsec.org/advisories/RUSTSEC-2026-0099
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |     [0m[36m├[0m Permitted subtree name constraints for DNS names were accepted for certificates asserting a wildcard name.
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |       
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |       This was incorrect because, given a name constraint of `accept.example.com`, `*.example.com` could feasibly allow a name of `reject.example.com` which is outside the constraint.
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |       This is very similar to [CVE-2025-61727](https://go.dev/issue/76442).
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |       
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |       Since name constraints are restrictions on otherwise properly-issued certificates, this bug is reachable only after signature verification and requires misissuance to exploit.
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |       
[rustls/audit                                          ]   |       This vulnerability is identified as [GHSA-xgp8-3hg3-c2mh](https://github.com/rustls/webpki/security/advisories/GHSA-xgp8-3hg3-c2mh). Thank you to @1seal for the report.

if [[ -z $toolchain ]]; then
  # GitHub does not enforce `required: true` inputs itself. https://github.com/actions/runner/issues/1070
  echo "'toolchain' is a required input" >&2
  exit 1
elif [[ $toolchain =~ ^stable' '[0-9]+' '(year|month|week|day)s?' 'ago$ ]]; then
  if [[ Linux == macOS ]]; then
    echo "toolchain=1.$((($(date -v-$(sed 's/stable \([0-9]*\) \(.\).*/\1\2/' <<< $toolchain) +%s)/60/60/24-16569)/7/6))" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
  else
    echo "toolchain=1.$((($(date --date "${toolchain#stable }" +%s)/60/60/24-16569)/7/6))" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
  fi
elif [[ $toolchain =~ ^stable' 'minus' '[0-9]+' 'releases?$ ]]; then
  echo "toolchain=1.$((($(date +%s)/60/60/24-16569)/7/6-${toolchain//[^0-9]/}))" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
elif [[ $toolchain =~ ^1\.[0-9]+$ ]]; then
  echo "toolchain=1.$((i=${toolchain#1.}, c=($(date +%s)/60/60/24-16569)/7/6, i+9*i*(10*i<=c)+90*i*(100*i<=c)))" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
else
  echo "toolchain=$toolchain" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
fi

[rustls/Features                                       ] ⭐ Run Main : parse toolchain version
if [[ -z $toolchain ]]; then
  # GitHub does not enforce `required: true` inputs itself. https://github.com/actions/runner/issues/1070
  echo "'toolchain' is a required input" >&2
  exit 1
elif [[ $toolchain =~ ^stable' '[0-9]+' '(year|month|week|day)s?' 'ago$ ]]; then
  if [[ Linux == macOS ]]; then
    echo "toolchain=1.$((($(date -v-$(sed 's/stable \([0-9]*\) \(.\).*/\1\2/' <<< $toolchain) +%s)/60/60/24-16569)/7/6))" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
  else
    echo "toolchain=1.$((($(date --date "${toolchain#stable }" +%s)/60/60/24-16569)/7/6))" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
  fi
elif [[ $toolchain =~ ^stable' 'minus' '[0-9]+' 'releases?$ ]]; then
  echo "toolchain=1.$((($(date +%s)/60/60/24-16569)/7/6-${toolchain//[^0-9]/}))" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
elif [[ $toolchain =~ ^1\.[0-9]+$ ]]; then
  echo "toolchain=1.$((i=${toolchain#1.}, c=($(date +%s)/60/60/24-16569)/7/6, i+9*i*(10*i<=c)+90*i*(100*i<=c)))" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
else
  echo "toolchain=$toolchain" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
fi

if [[ -z $toolchain ]]; then
  # GitHub does not enforce `required: true` inputs itself. https://github.com/actions/runner/issues/1070
  echo "'toolchain' is a required input" >&2
  exit 1
elif [[ $toolchain =~ ^stable' '[0-9]+' '(year|month|week|day)s?' 'ago$ ]]; then
  if [[ Linux == macOS ]]; then
    echo "toolchain=1.$((($(date -v-$(sed 's/stable \([0-9]*\) \(.\).*/\1\2/' <<< $toolchain) +%s)/60/60/24-16569)/7/6))" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
  else
    echo "toolchain=1.$((($(date --date "${toolchain#stable }" +%s)/60/60/24-16569)/7/6))" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
  fi
elif [[ $toolchain =~ ^stable' 'minus' '[0-9]+' 'releases?$ ]]; then
  echo "toolchain=1.$((($(date +%s)/60/60/24-16569)/7/6-${toolchain//[^0-9]/}))" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
elif [[ $toolchain =~ ^1\.[0-9]+$ ]]; then
  echo "toolchain=1.$((i=${toolchain#1.}, c=($(date +%s)/60/60/24-16569)/7/6, i+9*i*(10*i<=c)+90*i*(100*i<=c)))" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
else
  echo "toolchain=$toolchain" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
fi

if [[ -z $toolchain ]]; then
  # GitHub does not enforce `required: true` inputs itself. https://github.com/actions/runner/issues/1070
  echo "'toolchain' is a required input" >&2
  exit 1
elif [[ $toolchain =~ ^stable' '[0-9]+' '(year|month|week|day)s?' 'ago$ ]]; then
  if [[ Linux == macOS ]]; then
    echo "toolchain=1.$((($(date -v-$(sed 's/stable \([0-9]*\) \(.\).*/\1\2/' <<< $toolchain) +%s)/60/60/24-16569)/7/6))" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
  else
    echo "toolchain=1.$((($(date --date "${toolchain#stable }" +%s)/60/60/24-16569)/7/6))" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
  fi
elif [[ $toolchain =~ ^stable' 'minus' '[0-9]+' 'releases?$ ]]; then
  echo "toolchain=1.$((($(date +%s)/60/60/24-16569)/7/6-${toolchain//[^0-9]/}))" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
elif [[ $toolchain =~ ^1\.[0-9]+$ ]]; then
  echo "toolchain=1.$((i=${toolchain#1.}, c=($(date +%s)/60/60/24-16569)/7/6, i+9*i*(10*i<=c)+90*i*(100*i<=c)))" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
else
  echo "toolchain=$toolchain" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
fi

[rustls/Smoke-test fuzzing targets                     ] ⭐ Run Main : parse toolchain version
if [[ -z $toolchain ]]; then
  # GitHub does not enforce `required: true` inputs itself. https://github.com/actions/runner/issues/1070
  echo "'toolchain' is a required input" >&2
  exit 1
elif [[ $toolchain =~ ^stable' '[0-9]+' '(year|month|week|day)s?' 'ago$ ]]; then
  if [[ Linux == macOS ]]; then
    echo "toolchain=1.$((($(date -v-$(sed 's/stable \([0-9]*\) \(.\).*/\1\2/' <<< $toolchain) +%s)/60/60/24-16569)/7/6))" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
  else
    echo "toolchain=1.$((($(date --date "${toolchain#stable }" +%s)/60/60/24-16569)/7/6))" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
  fi
elif [[ $toolchain =~ ^stable' 'minus' '[0-9]+' 'releases?$ ]]; then
  echo "toolchain=1.$((($(date +%s)/60/60/24-16569)/7/6-${toolchain//[^0-9]/}))" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
elif [[ $toolchain =~ ^1\.[0-9]+$ ]]; then
  echo "toolchain=1.$((i=${toolchain#1.}, c=($(date +%s)/60/60/24-16569)/7/6, i+9*i*(10*i<=c)+90*i*(100*i<=c)))" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
else
  echo "toolchain=$toolchain" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
fi

[rustls/Check minimum versions of direct dependencies  ] ⭐ Run Main : parse toolchain version
if [[ -z $toolchain ]]; then
  # GitHub does not enforce `required: true` inputs itself. https://github.com/actions/runner/issues/1070
  echo "'toolchain' is a required input" >&2
  exit 1
elif [[ $toolchain =~ ^stable' '[0-9]+' '(year|month|week|day)s?' 'ago$ ]]; then
  if [[ Linux == macOS ]]; then
    echo "toolchain=1.$((($(date -v-$(sed 's/stable \([0-9]*\) \(.\).*/\1\2/' <<< $toolchain) +%s)/60/60/24-16569)/7/6))" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
  else
    echo "toolchain=1.$((($(date --date "${toolchain#stable }" +%s)/60/60/24-16569)/7/6))" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
  fi
elif [[ $toolchain =~ ^stable' 'minus' '[0-9]+' 'releases?$ ]]; then
  echo "toolchain=1.$((($(date +%s)/60/60/24-16569)/7/6-${toolchain//[^0-9]/}))" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
elif [[ $toolchain =~ ^1\.[0-9]+$ ]]; then
  echo "toolchain=1.$((i=${toolchain#1.}, c=($(date +%s)/60/60/24-16569)/7/6, i+9*i*(10*i<=c)+90*i*(100*i<=c)))" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
else
  echo "toolchain=$toolchain" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
fi

  # GitHub does not enforce `required: true` inputs itself. https://github.com/actions/runner/issues/1070
  echo "'toolchain' is a required input" >&2
  exit 1
elif [[ $toolchain =~ ^stable' '[0-9]+' '(year|month|week|day)s?' 'ago$ ]]; then
  if [[ Linux == macOS ]]; then
    echo "toolchain=1.$((($(date -v-$(sed 's/stable \([0-9]*\) \(.\).*/\1\2/' <<< $toolchain) +%s)/60/60/24-16569)/7/6))" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
  else
    echo "toolchain=1.$((($(date --date "${toolchain#stable }" +%s)/60/60/24-16569)/7/6))" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
  fi
elif [[ $toolchain =~ ^stable' 'minus' '[0-9]+' 'releases?$ ]]; then
  echo "toolchain=1.$((($(date +%s)/60/60/24-16569)/7/6-${toolchain//[^0-9]/}))" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
elif [[ $toolchain =~ ^1\.[0-9]+$ ]]; then
  echo "toolchain=1.$((i=${toolchain#1.}, c=($(date +%s)/60/60/24-16569)/7/6, i+9*i*(10*i<=c)+90*i*(100*i<=c)))" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
else
  echo "toolchain=$toolchain" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
fi

[rustls/Run openssl-tests                              ] ⭐ Run Main : enable Cargo sparse registry
# implemented in 1.66, stabilized in 1.68, made default in 1.70
if [ -z "${CARGO_REGISTRIES_CRATES_IO_PROTOCOL+set}" -o -f "/tmp"/.implicit_cargo_registries_crates_io_protocol ]; then
  if rustc +stable --version --verbose | grep -q '^release: 1\.6[89]\.'; then
    touch "/tmp"/.implicit_cargo_registries_crates_io_protocol || true
    echo CARGO_REGISTRIES_CRATES_IO_PROTOCOL=sparse >> $GITHUB_ENV
  elif rustc +stable --version --verbose | grep -q '^release: 1\.6[67]\.'; then
    touch "/tmp"/.implicit_cargo_registries_crates_io_protocol || true
    echo CARGO_REGISTRIES_CRATES_IO_PROTOCOL=git >> $GITHUB_ENV
  fi
fi

if [ -z "${CARGO_REGISTRIES_CRATES_IO_PROTOCOL+set}" -o -f "/tmp"/.implicit_cargo_registries_crates_io_protocol ]; then

[rustls/cross-target testing-4                         ] ⭐ Run Main : parse toolchain version
if [[ -z $toolchain ]]; then
  # GitHub does not enforce `required: true` inputs itself. https://github.com/actions/runner/issues/1070
  echo "'toolchain' is a required input" >&2
  exit 1
elif [[ $toolchain =~ ^stable' '[0-9]+' '(year|month|week|day)s?' 'ago$ ]]; then
  if [[ Linux == macOS ]]; then
    echo "toolchain=1.$((($(date -v-$(sed 's/stable \([0-9]*\) \(.\).*/\1\2/' <<< $toolchain) +%s)/60/60/24-16569)/7/6))" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
  else
    echo "toolchain=1.$((($(date --date "${toolchain#stable }" +%s)/60/60/24-16569)/7/6))" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
  fi
elif [[ $toolchain =~ ^stable' 'minus' '[0-9]+' 'releases?$ ]]; then
  echo "toolchain=1.$((($(date +%s)/60/60/24-16569)/7/6-${toolchain//[^0-9]/}))" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
elif [[ $toolchain =~ ^1\.[0-9]+$ ]]; then
  echo "toolchain=1.$((i=${toolchain#1.}, c=($(date +%s)/60/60/24-16569)/7/6, i+9*i*(10*i<=c)+90*i*(100*i<=c)))" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
else
  echo "toolchain=$toolchain" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
fi

[rustls/cross-target testing-3                         ] ⭐ Run Main : parse toolchain version
if [[ -z $toolchain ]]; then
  # GitHub does not enforce `required: true` inputs itself. https://github.com/actions/runner/issues/1070
  echo "'toolchain' is a required input" >&2
  exit 1
elif [[ $toolchain =~ ^stable' '[0-9]+' '(year|month|week|day)s?' 'ago$ ]]; then
  if [[ Linux == macOS ]]; then
    echo "toolchain=1.$((($(date -v-$(sed 's/stable \([0-9]*\) \(.\).*/\1\2/' <<< $toolchain) +%s)/60/60/24-16569)/7/6))" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
  else
    echo "toolchain=1.$((($(date --date "${toolchain#stable }" +%s)/60/60/24-16569)/7/6))" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
  fi
elif [[ $toolchain =~ ^stable' 'minus' '[0-9]+' 'releases?$ ]]; then
  echo "toolchain=1.$((($(date +%s)/60/60/24-16569)/7/6-${toolchain//[^0-9]/}))" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
elif [[ $toolchain =~ ^1\.[0-9]+$ ]]; then
  echo "toolchain=1.$((i=${toolchain#1.}, c=($(date +%s)/60/60/24-16569)/7/6, i+9*i*(10*i<=c)+90*i*(100*i<=c)))" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
else
  echo "toolchain=$toolchain" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
fi

[rustls/Measure coverage                               ] ⭐ Run Main : parse toolchain version
if [[ -z $toolchain ]]; then
  # GitHub does not enforce `required: true` inputs itself. https://github.com/actions/runner/issues/1070
  echo "'toolchain' is a required input" >&2
  exit 1
elif [[ $toolchain =~ ^stable' '[0-9]+' '(year|month|week|day)s?' 'ago$ ]]; then
  if [[ Linux == macOS ]]; then
    echo "toolchain=1.$((($(date -v-$(sed 's/stable \([0-9]*\) \(.\).*/\1\2/' <<< $toolchain) +%s)/60/60/24-16569)/7/6))" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
  else
    echo "toolchain=1.$((($(date --date "${toolchain#stable }" +%s)/60/60/24-16569)/7/6))" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
  fi
elif [[ $toolchain =~ ^stable' 'minus' '[0-9]+' 'releases?$ ]]; then
  echo "toolchain=1.$((($(date +%s)/60/60/24-16569)/7/6-${toolchain//[^0-9]/}))" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
elif [[ $toolchain =~ ^1\.[0-9]+$ ]]; then
  echo "toolchain=1.$((i=${toolchain#1.}, c=($(date +%s)/60/60/24-16569)/7/6, i+9*i*(10*i<=c)+90*i*(100*i<=c)))" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
else
  echo "toolchain=$toolchain" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
fi

[rustls/Taplo                                          ] ⭐ Run Main set -eu
if ! command -v bash >/dev/null; then
  if grep -Eq '^ID=alpine' /etc/os-release; then
    printf '::group::Install packages required for cache-cargo-install-action (bash)\n'
    # NB: sync with apk_install in pre.sh
    if command -v sudo >/dev/null; then
      sudo apk --no-cache add bash
    elif command -v doas >/dev/null; then
      doas apk --no-cache add bash
    else
      apk --no-cache add bash
    fi
    printf '::endgroup::\n'
  else
    printf '::error::cache-cargo-install-action requires bash\n'
    exit 1
  fi
fi

[rustls/Smoke-test fuzzing targets                     ] ⭐ Run Main : enable Cargo sparse registry
# implemented in 1.66, stabilized in 1.68, made default in 1.70
if [ -z "${CARGO_REGISTRIES_CRATES_IO_PROTOCOL+set}" -o -f "/tmp"/.implicit_cargo_registries_crates_io_protocol ]; then
  if rustc +stable --version --verbose | grep -q '^release: 1\.6[89]\.'; then
    touch "/tmp"/.implicit_cargo_registries_crates_io_protocol || true
    echo CARGO_REGISTRIES_CRATES_IO_PROTOCOL=sparse >> $GITHUB_ENV
  elif rustc +stable --version --verbose | grep -q '^release: 1\.6[67]\.'; then
    touch "/tmp"/.implicit_cargo_registries_crates_io_protocol || true
    echo CARGO_REGISTRIES_CRATES_IO_PROTOCOL=git >> $GITHUB_ENV
  fi
fi

fi

if ! command -v bash >/dev/null; then
  if grep -Eq '^ID=alpine' /etc/os-release; then
    printf '::group::Install packages required for cache-cargo-install-action (bash)\n'
    # NB: sync with apk_install in pre.sh
    if command -v sudo >/dev/null; then
      sudo apk --no-cache add bash
    elif command -v doas >/dev/null; then
      doas apk --no-cache add bash
    else
      apk --no-cache add bash
    fi
    printf '::endgroup::\n'
  else
    printf '::error::cache-cargo-install-action requires bash\n'
    exit 1
  fi
fi

      doas apk --no-cache add bash
    else
      apk --no-cache add bash
    fi
    printf '::endgroup::\n'
  else
    printf '::error::cache-cargo-install-action requires bash\n'
    exit 1
  fi
fi

[rustls/Check minimum versions of direct dependencies  ] ⭐ Run Main : enable Cargo sparse registry
# implemented in 1.66, stabilized in 1.68, made default in 1.70
if [ -z "${CARGO_REGISTRIES_CRATES_IO_PROTOCOL+set}" -o -f "/tmp"/.implicit_cargo_registries_crates_io_protocol ]; then
  if rustc +stable --version --verbose | grep -q '^release: 1\.6[89]\.'; then
    touch "/tmp"/.implicit_cargo_registries_crates_io_protocol || true
    echo CARGO_REGISTRIES_CRATES_IO_PROTOCOL=sparse >> $GITHUB_ENV
  elif rustc +stable --version --verbose | grep -q '^release: 1\.6[67]\.'; then
    touch "/tmp"/.implicit_cargo_registries_crates_io_protocol || true
    echo CARGO_REGISTRIES_CRATES_IO_PROTOCOL=git >> $GITHUB_ENV
  fi
fi

[rustls/Check minimum versions of direct dependencies  ] ⭐ Run Main set -eu
if ! command -v bash >/dev/null; then
  if grep -Eq '^ID=alpine' /etc/os-release; then
    printf '::group::Install packages required for install-action (bash)\n'
    # NB: sync with apk_install in main.sh
    if command -v sudo >/dev/null; then
      sudo apk --no-cache add bash
    elif command -v doas >/dev/null; then
      doas apk --no-cache add bash
    else
      apk --no-cache add bash
    fi
    printf '::endgroup::\n'
  else
    printf '::error::install-action requires bash\n'
    exit 1
  fi
fi

[rustls/Check minimum versions of direct dependencies  ] ⭐ Run Main set -eu
if ! command -v bash >/dev/null; then
  if grep -Eq '^ID=alpine' /etc/os-release; then
    printf '::group::Install packages required for install-action (bash)\n'
    # NB: sync with apk_install in main.sh
    if command -v sudo >/dev/null; then
      sudo apk --no-cache add bash
    elif command -v doas >/dev/null; then
      doas apk --no-cache add bash
    else
      apk --no-cache add bash
    fi
    printf '::endgroup::\n'
  else
    printf '::error::install-action requires bash\n'
    exit 1
  fi
fi

# implemented in 1.66, stabilized in 1.68, made default in 1.70
if [ -z "${CARGO_REGISTRIES_CRATES_IO_PROTOCOL+set}" -o -f "/tmp"/.implicit_cargo_registries_crates_io_protocol ]; then
  if rustc +stable --version --verbose | grep -q '^release: 1\.6[89]\.'; then
    touch "/tmp"/.implicit_cargo_registries_crates_io_protocol || true
    echo CARGO_REGISTRIES_CRATES_IO_PROTOCOL=sparse >> $GITHUB_ENV
  elif rustc +stable --version --verbose | grep -q '^release: 1\.6[67]\.'; then
    touch "/tmp"/.implicit_cargo_registries_crates_io_protocol || true
    echo CARGO_REGISTRIES_CRATES_IO_PROTOCOL=git >> $GITHUB_ENV
  fi
fi

if ! command -v bash >/dev/null; then
  if grep -Eq '^ID=alpine' /etc/os-release; then
    printf '::group::Install packages required for cache-cargo-install-action (bash)\n'
    # NB: sync with apk_install in pre.sh
    if command -v sudo >/dev/null; then
      sudo apk --no-cache add bash
    elif command -v doas >/dev/null; then
      doas apk --no-cache add bash
    else
      apk --no-cache add bash
    fi
    printf '::endgroup::\n'
  else
    printf '::error::cache-cargo-install-action requires bash\n'
    exit 1
  fi
fi

if ! command -v bash >/dev/null; then
  if grep -Eq '^ID=alpine' /etc/os-release; then
    printf '::group::Install packages required for cache-cargo-install-action (bash)\n'
    # NB: sync with apk_install in pre.sh
    if command -v sudo >/dev/null; then
      sudo apk --no-cache add bash
    elif command -v doas >/dev/null; then
      doas apk --no-cache add bash
    else
      apk --no-cache add bash
    fi
    printf '::endgroup::\n'
  else
    printf '::error::cache-cargo-install-action requires bash\n'
    exit 1
  fi
fi

[rustls/cross-target testing-4                         ] ⭐ Run Main set -eu
if ! command -v bash >/dev/null; then
  if grep -Eq '^ID=alpine' /etc/os-release; then
    printf '::group::Install packages required for cache-cargo-install-action (bash)\n'
    # NB: sync with apk_install in pre.sh
    if command -v sudo >/dev/null; then
      sudo apk --no-cache add bash
    elif command -v doas >/dev/null; then
      doas apk --no-cache add bash
    else
      apk --no-cache add bash
    fi
    printf '::endgroup::\n'
  else
    printf '::error::cache-cargo-install-action requires bash\n'
    exit 1
  fi

if ! command -v bash >/dev/null; then
  if grep -Eq '^ID=alpine' /etc/os-release; then
    printf '::group::Install packages required for cache-cargo-install-action (bash)\n'
    # NB: sync with apk_install in pre.sh
    if command -v sudo >/dev/null; then
      sudo apk --no-cache add bash
    elif command -v doas >/dev/null; then
      doas apk --no-cache add bash
    else
      apk --no-cache add bash
    fi
    printf '::endgroup::\n'
  else
    printf '::error::cache-cargo-install-action requires bash\n'
    exit 1
  fi
fi

[rustls/Build+test                                     ] ⭐ Run Main : enable Cargo sparse registry
# implemented in 1.66, stabilized in 1.68, made default in 1.70
if [ -z "${CARGO_REGISTRIES_CRATES_IO_PROTOCOL+set}" -o -f "/tmp"/.implicit_cargo_registries_crates_io_protocol ]; then
  if rustc +stable --version --verbose | grep -q '^release: 1\.6[89]\.'; then
    touch "/tmp"/.implicit_cargo_registries_crates_io_protocol || true
    echo CARGO_REGISTRIES_CRATES_IO_PROTOCOL=sparse >> $GITHUB_ENV
  elif rustc +stable --version --verbose | grep -q '^release: 1\.6[67]\.'; then
    touch "/tmp"/.implicit_cargo_registries_crates_io_protocol || true
    echo CARGO_REGISTRIES_CRATES_IO_PROTOCOL=git >> $GITHUB_ENV
  fi
fi

[rustls/Build+test                                     ]   ✅  Success - Main : enable Cargo sparse registry
# implemented in 1.66, stabilized in 1.68, made default in 1.70
if [ -z "${CARGO_REGISTRIES_CRATES_IO_PROTOCOL+set}" -o -f "/tmp"/.implicit_cargo_registries_crates_io_protocol ]; then
  if rustc +stable --version --verbose | grep -q '^release: 1\.6[89]\.'; then
    touch "/tmp"/.implicit_cargo_registries_crates_io_protocol || true
    echo CARGO_REGISTRIES_CRATES_IO_PROTOCOL=sparse >> $GITHUB_ENV
  elif rustc +stable --version --verbose | grep -q '^release: 1\.6[67]\.'; then
    touch "/tmp"/.implicit_cargo_registries_crates_io_protocol || true
    echo CARGO_REGISTRIES_CRATES_IO_PROTOCOL=git >> $GITHUB_ENV
  fi
fi

# implemented in 1.66, stabilized in 1.68, made default in 1.70
if [ -z "${CARGO_REGISTRIES_CRATES_IO_PROTOCOL+set}" -o -f "/tmp"/.implicit_cargo_registries_crates_io_protocol ]; then
  if rustc +stable --version --verbose | grep -q '^release: 1\.6[89]\.'; then
    touch "/tmp"/.implicit_cargo_registries_crates_io_protocol || true
    echo CARGO_REGISTRIES_CRATES_IO_PROTOCOL=sparse >> $GITHUB_ENV
  elif rustc +stable --version --verbose | grep -q '^release: 1\.6[67]\.'; then
    touch "/tmp"/.implicit_cargo_registries_crates_io_protocol || true
    echo CARGO_REGISTRIES_CRATES_IO_PROTOCOL=git >> $GITHUB_ENV
  fi
fi

# implemented in 1.66, stabilized in 1.68, made default in 1.70
if [ -z "${CARGO_REGISTRIES_CRATES_IO_PROTOCOL+set}" -o -f "/tmp"/.implicit_cargo_registries_crates_io_protocol ]; then
  if rustc +nightly --version --verbose | grep -q '^release: 1\.6[89]\.'; then
    touch "/tmp"/.implicit_cargo_registries_crates_io_protocol || true
    echo CARGO_REGISTRIES_CRATES_IO_PROTOCOL=sparse >> $GITHUB_ENV
  elif rustc +nightly --version --verbose | grep -q '^release: 1\.6[67]\.'; then
    touch "/tmp"/.implicit_cargo_registries_crates_io_protocol || true
    echo CARGO_REGISTRIES_CRATES_IO_PROTOCOL=git >> $GITHUB_ENV
  fi
fi

[rustls/cross-target testing-3                         ] ⭐ Run Main set -eu
if ! command -v bash >/dev/null; then
  if grep -Eq '^ID=alpine' /etc/os-release; then
    printf '::group::Install packages required for cache-cargo-install-action (bash)\n'
    # NB: sync with apk_install in pre.sh
    if command -v sudo >/dev/null; then
      sudo apk --no-cache add bash
    elif command -v doas >/dev/null; then
      doas apk --no-cache add bash
    else
      apk --no-cache add bash
    fi
    printf '::endgroup::\n'
  else
    printf '::error::cache-cargo-install-action requires bash\n'
    exit 1
  fi
fi

if ! command -v bash >/dev/null; then
  if grep -Eq '^ID=alpine' /etc/os-release; then
    printf '::group::Install packages required for cache-cargo-install-action (bash)\n'
    # NB: sync with apk_install in pre.sh
    if command -v sudo >/dev/null; then
      sudo apk --no-cache add bash
    elif command -v doas >/dev/null; then
      doas apk --no-cache add bash
    else
      apk --no-cache add bash
    fi
    printf '::endgroup::\n'
  else
    printf '::error::cache-cargo-install-action requires bash\n'
    exit 1
  fi
fi

[rustls/Measure coverage                               ] ⭐ Run Main : enable Cargo sparse registry
# implemented in 1.66, stabilized in 1.68, made default in 1.70
if [ -z "${CARGO_REGISTRIES_CRATES_IO_PROTOCOL+set}" -o -f "/tmp"/.implicit_cargo_registries_crates_io_protocol ]; then
  if rustc +nightly --version --verbose | grep -q '^release: 1\.6[89]\.'; then
    touch "/tmp"/.implicit_cargo_registries_crates_io_protocol || true
    echo CARGO_REGISTRIES_CRATES_IO_PROTOCOL=sparse >> $GITHUB_ENV
  elif rustc +nightly --version --verbose | grep -q '^release: 1\.6[67]\.'; then
    touch "/tmp"/.implicit_cargo_registries_crates_io_protocol || true
    echo CARGO_REGISTRIES_CRATES_IO_PROTOCOL=git >> $GITHUB_ENV
  fi
fi

[rustls/Measure coverage                               ] ⭐ Run Main set -eu
if ! command -v bash >/dev/null; then
  if grep -Eq '^ID=alpine' /etc/os-release; then
    printf '::group::Install packages required for cache-cargo-install-action (bash)\n'
    # NB: sync with apk_install in pre.sh
    if command -v sudo >/dev/null; then
      sudo apk --no-cache add bash
    elif command -v doas >/dev/null; then
      doas apk --no-cache add bash
    else
      apk --no-cache add bash
    fi
    printf '::endgroup::\n'
  else
    printf '::error::cache-cargo-install-action requires bash\n'
    exit 1
  fi
fi

[rustls/Smoke-test fuzzing targets                     ]   | #423307	REDUCE cov: 809 ft: 1409 corp: 392/11739b lim: 1210 exec/s: 70551 rss: 182Mb L: 25/273 MS: 2 InsertByte-EraseBytes-
[rustls/Clippy                                         ] ⭐ Run Main : parse toolchain version
if [[ -z $toolchain ]]; then
  # GitHub does not enforce `required: true` inputs itself. https://github.com/actions/runner/issues/1070
  echo "'toolchain' is a required input" >&2
  exit 1
elif [[ $toolchain =~ ^stable' '[0-9]+' '(year|month|week|day)s?' 'ago$ ]]; then
  if [[ Linux == macOS ]]; then
    echo "toolchain=1.$((($(date -v-$(sed 's/stable \([0-9]*\) \(.\).*/\1\2/' <<< $toolchain) +%s)/60/60/24-16569)/7/6))" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
  else
    echo "toolchain=1.$((($(date --date "${toolchain#stable }" +%s)/60/60/24-16569)/7/6))" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
  fi
elif [[ $toolchain =~ ^stable' 'minus' '[0-9]+' 'releases?$ ]]; then
  echo "toolchain=1.$((($(date +%s)/60/60/24-16569)/7/6-${toolchain//[^0-9]/}))" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
elif [[ $toolchain =~ ^1\.[0-9]+$ ]]; then
  echo "toolchain=1.$((i=${toolchain#1.}, c=($(date +%s)/60/60/24-16569)/7/6, i+9*i*(10*i<=c)+90*i*(100*i<=c)))" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
else
  echo "toolchain=$toolchain" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
fi

[rustls/Clippy                                         ]   ✅  Success - Main : parse toolchain version
if [[ -z $toolchain ]]; then
  # GitHub does not enforce `required: true` inputs itself. https://github.com/actions/runner/issues/1070
  echo "'toolchain' is a required input" >&2
  exit 1
elif [[ $toolchain =~ ^stable' '[0-9]+' '(year|month|week|day)s?' 'ago$ ]]; then
  if [[ Linux == macOS ]]; then
    echo "toolchain=1.$((($(date -v-$(sed 's/stable \([0-9]*\) \(.\).*/\1\2/' <<< $toolchain) +%s)/60/60/24-16569)/7/6))" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
  else
    echo "toolchain=1.$((($(date --date "${toolchain#stable }" +%s)/60/60/24-16569)/7/6))" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
  fi
elif [[ $toolchain =~ ^stable' 'minus' '[0-9]+' 'releases?$ ]]; then
  echo "toolchain=1.$((($(date +%s)/60/60/24-16569)/7/6-${toolchain//[^0-9]/}))" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
elif [[ $toolchain =~ ^1\.[0-9]+$ ]]; then
  echo "toolchain=1.$((i=${toolchain#1.}, c=($(date +%s)/60/60/24-16569)/7/6, i+9*i*(10*i<=c)+90*i*(100*i<=c)))" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
else
  echo "toolchain=$toolchain" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
fi

if ! command -v rustup &>/dev/null; then
  curl --proto '=https' --tlsv1.2 --retry 10 --retry-connrefused --location --silent --show-error --fail https://sh.rustup.rs | sh -s -- --default-toolchain none -y

fi

  curl --proto '=https' --tlsv1.2 --retry 10 --retry-connrefused --location --silent --show-error --fail https://sh.rustup.rs | sh -s -- --default-toolchain none -y
  echo "$CARGO_HOME/bin" >> $GITHUB_PATH
fi

[rustls/Clippy                                         ] ⭐ Run Main : enable Cargo sparse registry
# implemented in 1.66, stabilized in 1.68, made default in 1.70
if [ -z "${CARGO_REGISTRIES_CRATES_IO_PROTOCOL+set}" -o -f "/tmp"/.implicit_cargo_registries_crates_io_protocol ]; then
  if rustc +stable --version --verbose | grep -q '^release: 1\.6[89]\.'; then
    touch "/tmp"/.implicit_cargo_registries_crates_io_protocol || true
    echo CARGO_REGISTRIES_CRATES_IO_PROTOCOL=sparse >> $GITHUB_ENV
  elif rustc +stable --version --verbose | grep -q '^release: 1\.6[67]\.'; then
    touch "/tmp"/.implicit_cargo_registries_crates_io_protocol || true
    echo CARGO_REGISTRIES_CRATES_IO_PROTOCOL=git >> $GITHUB_ENV
  fi
fi

    touch "/tmp"/.implicit_cargo_registries_crates_io_protocol || true
    echo CARGO_REGISTRIES_CRATES_IO_PROTOCOL=sparse >> $GITHUB_ENV
  elif rustc +stable --version --verbose | grep -q '^release: 1\.6[67]\.'; then
    touch "/tmp"/.implicit_cargo_registries_crates_io_protocol || true
    echo CARGO_REGISTRIES_CRATES_IO_PROTOCOL=git >> $GITHUB_ENV
  fi
fi

[rustls/BoGo test suite                                ]   |

[rustls/BoGo test suite                                ]   |   git switch -

if [[ -z $toolchain ]]; then
  # GitHub does not enforce `required: true` inputs itself. https://github.com/actions/runner/issues/1070
  echo "'toolchain' is a required input" >&2
  exit 1
elif [[ $toolchain =~ ^stable' '[0-9]+' '(year|month|week|day)s?' 'ago$ ]]; then
  if [[ Linux == macOS ]]; then
    echo "toolchain=1.$((($(date -v-$(sed 's/stable \([0-9]*\) \(.\).*/\1\2/' <<< $toolchain) +%s)/60/60/24-16569)/7/6))" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
  else
    echo "toolchain=1.$((($(date --date "${toolchain#stable }" +%s)/60/60/24-16569)/7/6))" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
  fi
elif [[ $toolchain =~ ^stable' 'minus' '[0-9]+' 'releases?$ ]]; then
  echo "toolchain=1.$((($(date +%s)/60/60/24-16569)/7/6-${toolchain//[^0-9]/}))" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
elif [[ $toolchain =~ ^1\.[0-9]+$ ]]; then
  echo "toolchain=1.$((i=${toolchain#1.}, c=($(date +%s)/60/60/24-16569)/7/6, i+9*i*(10*i<=c)+90*i*(100*i<=c)))" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
else
  echo "toolchain=$toolchain" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
fi

if [[ -z $toolchain ]]; then
  # GitHub does not enforce `required: true` inputs itself. https://github.com/actions/runner/issues/1070
  echo "'toolchain' is a required input" >&2
  exit 1
elif [[ $toolchain =~ ^stable' '[0-9]+' '(year|month|week|day)s?' 'ago$ ]]; then
  if [[ Linux == macOS ]]; then
    echo "toolchain=1.$((($(date -v-$(sed 's/stable \([0-9]*\) \(.\).*/\1\2/' <<< $toolchain) +%s)/60/60/24-16569)/7/6))" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
  else
    echo "toolchain=1.$((($(date --date "${toolchain#stable }" +%s)/60/60/24-16569)/7/6))" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
  fi
elif [[ $toolchain =~ ^stable' 'minus' '[0-9]+' 'releases?$ ]]; then
  echo "toolchain=1.$((($(date +%s)/60/60/24-16569)/7/6-${toolchain//[^0-9]/}))" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
elif [[ $toolchain =~ ^1\.[0-9]+$ ]]; then
  echo "toolchain=1.$((i=${toolchain#1.}, c=($(date +%s)/60/60/24-16569)/7/6, i+9*i*(10*i<=c)+90*i*(100*i<=c)))" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
else
  echo "toolchain=$toolchain" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
fi

# implemented in 1.66, stabilized in 1.68, made default in 1.70
if [ -z "${CARGO_REGISTRIES_CRATES_IO_PROTOCOL+set}" -o -f "/tmp"/.implicit_cargo_registries_crates_io_protocol ]; then
  if rustc +stable --version --verbose | grep -q '^release: 1\.6[89]\.'; then
    touch "/tmp"/.implicit_cargo_registries_crates_io_protocol || true
    echo CARGO_REGISTRIES_CRATES_IO_PROTOCOL=sparse >> $GITHUB_ENV
  elif rustc +stable --version --verbose | grep -q '^release: 1\.6[67]\.'; then
    touch "/tmp"/.implicit_cargo_registries_crates_io_protocol || true
    echo CARGO_REGISTRIES_CRATES_IO_PROTOCOL=git >> $GITHUB_ENV
  fi
fi

[rustls/cross-target testing-8                         ] ⭐ Run Main set -eu
if ! command -v bash >/dev/null; then
  if grep -Eq '^ID=alpine' /etc/os-release; then
    printf '::group::Install packages required for cache-cargo-install-action (bash)\n'
    # NB: sync with apk_install in pre.sh
    if command -v sudo >/dev/null; then
      sudo apk --no-cache add bash
    elif command -v doas >/dev/null; then
      doas apk --no-cache add bash
    else
      apk --no-cache add bash
    fi
    printf '::endgroup::\n'
  else
    printf '::error::cache-cargo-install-action requires bash\n'
    exit 1
  fi
fi

[rustls/cross-target testing-5                         ]   ✅  Success - Main set -eu
if ! command -v bash >/dev/null; then
  if grep -Eq '^ID=alpine' /etc/os-release; then
    printf '::group::Install packages required for cache-cargo-install-action (bash)\n'
    # NB: sync with apk_install in pre.sh
    if command -v sudo >/dev/null; then
      sudo apk --no-cache add bash
    elif command -v doas >/dev/null; then
      doas apk --no-cache add bash
    else
      apk --no-cache add bash
    fi
    printf '::endgroup::\n'
  else
    printf '::error::cache-cargo-install-action requires bash\n'
    exit 1
  fi
fi

[rustls/cross-target testing-7                         ] ⭐ Run Main set -eu
if ! command -v bash >/dev/null; then
  if grep -Eq '^ID=alpine' /etc/os-release; then
    printf '::group::Install packages required for cache-cargo-install-action (bash)\n'
    # NB: sync with apk_install in pre.sh
    if command -v sudo >/dev/null; then
      sudo apk --no-cache add bash
    elif command -v doas >/dev/null; then
      doas apk --no-cache add bash
    else
      apk --no-cache add bash
    fi
    printf '::endgroup::\n'
  else
    printf '::error::cache-cargo-install-action requires bash\n'
    exit 1
  fi
fi
